article_meta.title: After some 150 students walked out of Redlands schools early this month in support of immigrants, they were dealt an une

reasoning: Mostly neutral paraphrase with one notable lapse: it labels policies as anti-LGBTQ+ without attribution and occasionally echoes the article’s contrast framing. Otherwise, it attributes claims and includes multiple perspectives.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses phrases like contrasts sharply and threatening severe consequences, mirroring article tone in places.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Most claims are grounded in the article; minor generalizations about supportive districts are acceptable.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Calls board policies anti-LGBTQ+ without the article’s attribution (‘what they regard as’).
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tone is measured; limited heightened language.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No attributions of intent or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Slight emphasis on student perspective but includes district’s viewpoint-neutral claim.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits some contextual details (e.g., specific incidents in LA) but covers main perspectives.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: anti-LGBTQ+ is a subjective label when not attributed.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically charged terms (anti-LGBTQ+) and contrast language appear.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Mild critical tilt toward Redlands’ response; not excessive.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Balances officials, students, and civil liberties group without privileging elites.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source overall but adopts one key piece of loaded framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Sources list mixes outlet and actors; misses some distinct perspectives (e.g., other district officials, Texas/Florida leaders).
